_meta.editorial_owner: Thomas W. Hilgers, MD (historical canon); Naomi M. Whittaker, MD (contemporary)

evidence_tier_definitions.peer_reviewed: Published in a peer-reviewed journal, verified PMID/DOI.
evidence_tier_definitions.clinic_presentation: Clinic document, slide deck, or non-peer-reviewed manuscript. Usable with caveat.
evidence_tier_definitions.clinical_experience: Clinician-attributed statement without a traceable document. Cite with explicit framing.
evidence_tier_definitions.organizational_primary: FEMM/RHRI, Saint Paul VI, clinic fact sheet, or NeoFertility institutional document (not peer-reviewed article, not clinician-attributed). Cite with document name and date.

facts.claim: Michael (1988) statistical analysis identified widespread introduction of technological contraceptives (oral contraceptives, IUDs, and later sterilization) as the major variable accounting for the U.S. divorce rate increase beginning in the 1960s, not divorce law changes, unemployment, or income variables.

source.title: Chapter 1: Disturbing Trends in the Health Care of Women, Children and Families
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The major variable that accounted for the increase in divorce was the introduction, on a widespread scale, of the various contraceptive" Section: The Divorce Rate.

facts.claim: Women who have used oral contraceptives show an increased risk of invasive cervical cancer, a risk related to the epidemic in human papilloma virus cervical infection (Moreno et al., IARC Multi-centric Case-Control Study, Lancet 2002).

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the increased relative risk of breast cancer in women who have long-term oral contraceptive use seems to be incontrovertible" Section: Oral Contraceptives and Cancer Risk.

facts.claim: The 3C'S MCSS (Consistency, Color, Change, Sensation Mucus Cycle Scoring System) was developed within NaProTECHNOLOGY to objectively assess the mucus cycle.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A consistency, color, change, and sensation mucus cycle scoring system (3C'S MCSS) has been developed to assist this process" Section: Overview.

facts.claim: Mean mucus cycle scores differed significantly across five classifications (regular, intermediate regular, limited, intermediate limited, dry), with p-values ranging from <.001 to <.01.

source.title: Chapter 14: Objective Classification of the Mucus Cycle
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The mean mucus cycle scores were significantly different for each of the five classifications (with p-scores ranging from <.001 to <.01)." Section: Validation Study.

facts.claim: Mucus cycles are classified into five types: regular, intermediate regular, intermediate limited, limited, and dry, based on decreasing mucus discharge amount.

source.title: Chapter 14: Objective Classification of the Mucus Cycle
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The mucus cycle was interpreted to be regular, limited, or dry based upon the observation of a decreasing amount of mucus discharge." Section: Validation Study.

facts.claim: The 3C'S MCSS is physician-independent because it relies solely on vulvar mucus observations made by the woman herself, not cervical examination.

source.title: Chapter 14: Objective Classification of the Mucus Cycle
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This makes the system physician-independent and gives CrMS users a greater opportunity to be health care participants." Section: Clinical Significance.

facts.claim: In the 3C'S MCSS, point values are assigned to the four major mucus observation components so that the score increases as fertility increases.

source.title: Chapter 14: Objective Classification of the Mucus Cycle
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "point values are assigned to the four major components...in such a fashion as to allow for an increasing score as fertility increases" Section: Scoring Methodology.

facts.claim: The Pope Paul VI Institute prospectively evaluated 1,914 consecutive menstrual and ovulation cycles at its Reproductive Ultrasound Center between September 1985 and May 2001.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This study involved the prospective evaluation of 1,914 consecutive menstrual and ovulation cycles" Section: Study Overview.

facts.claim: Anatomically normal ovulation by ultrasound requires a mature follicle with MFD >= 1.90 cm, presence of a cumulus oophorus, and complete rupture with corpus luteum formation over 24 hours.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A follicle is sonographically observed to develop and reach maturity (defined as a MFD greater than or equal to 1.90 cm)" Section: Anatomically Normal Ovulation.

facts.claim: A partial rupture of the follicle is defined sonographically as a follicular collapse of no more than 7.5 mm.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In a partial rupture of the follicle, the follicle collapses by no more than 7.5 mm." Section: Partial Rupture.

facts.claim: An unruptured follicle is identified when the follicle fills with internal echoes for two consecutive days, often enlarges, and the endometrium transitions from late proliferative to early secretory.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In unruptured follicles, the follicle fills in with internal echoes. This appearance is documented for two consecutive days." Section: Unruptured Follicle.

facts.claim: An immature follicle syndrome (IFS) is defined as complete rupture of a follicle whose MFD never reaches 1.90 cm; the cumulus may be present, absent, or retained.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "An immature follicle (with a MFD less than 1.90 cm)." Section: Immature Follicle (IFS).

facts.claim: Delayed rupture syndrome (DRS) is sonographically defined as follicular rupture occurring over a 48-hour period, with the initial 24 hours showing an MFD reduction of less than 7.5 mm followed by completion in the next 24 hours.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The follicle is observed to rupture over a 48-hour period." Section: Delayed Rupture of the Follicle (DRS).

facts.claim: An afollicular cycle (AF) is defined as a cycle in which no follicle exceeds an MFD of 1.4 cm, never reaching the 1.5 cm threshold for dominance.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "this follicle never reaches an MFD of greater than 1.4 cm whereas a dominant follicle begins at 1.5 cm" Section: Afollicularism (AF).

facts.claim: Of 1,914 follicular ultrasound series initiated, 1,818 were available for evaluation and 96 were never completed.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "1,818 series were available for evaluation - 96 were never completed" Section: Study Results.

facts.claim: Of evaluable cycles, 1,172 were spontaneous and 646 were medicated using agents such as Clomid, Pergonal, Metrodin, or GnRH.

source.title: Chapter 20: Disorders of Human Ovulation: Sonographic Classification System
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "1,172 were spontaneous cycles - 646 were medicated cycles (Clomid, Pergonal, Metrodin, GnRH, etc.)" Section: Study Results.

facts.claim: In the 460-cycle subfertility subgroup, patient ages ranged from 22 to 42 years with a mean age of 30.7 years.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a group of 57 patients were chosen to undergo daily follicular ultrasound studies." Section: Establishing Normal Controls.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: The P+7 endocrine panel measured in normal controls includes androstenedione, DHEAs, prolactin, testosterone, free testosterone, T4, T3 uptake, free thyroxine index, TSH (3rd generation), FSH, and LH.

source.title: Chapter 24: Establishing Normal Hormone Levels
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: P+7 blood draws correspond to the mid-luteal phase and are used as the standard single-timepoint endocrine assessment in NaPro.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.title: Chapter 24: Establishing Normal Hormone Levels
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "one standard deviation away from the mean is identified by the dashed lines. In this fashion, four zones of estradiol production can be identified." Section: Periovulatory Estradiol Profile.

facts.claim: Stress has a significant impact on ovulation and menstrual cycles, with effects from both physical and emotional stressors that may be acute or chronic.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Stress has an enormous impact on the ovulation and menstrual cycles. The stress may be physical or emotional." Section: Overview.

facts.claim: In Figure 28-3, a patient initiating a 45-minute jazzercise program with sit-ups and leg lifts plus a weight reduction diet (losing 10 pounds, from 122 to 112 lbs) developed completely dry cycles across three consecutive cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "45-minute jazzercise program with sit-ups and leg lifts...weight reduction diet and lost 10 pounds (from 122 lbs. to 112 lbs.)" Section: Figure 28-3.

facts.claim: In the Figure 28-3 case, when strenuous exercise and dieting were discontinued, normal mucus cycles returned and the patient subsequently achieved pregnancy resulting in a live-born baby boy.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When she did this, she reverted back to having normal mucus cycles. A few cycles later, she became pregnant" Section: Figure 28-3.

facts.claim: In a wedding-stress case (Figure 28-4), the first cycle (37 days) showed a classic double Peak with first Peak on day 12 and a second mucus buildup days 18-26.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a 37-day cycle...Peak Day on day 12 and then a second mucus buildup beginning on day 18 and concluding on day 26." Section: Figure 28-4.

facts.claim: In Figure 28-4 second cycle (40 days), prolonged mucus buildup concluded on day 32 with a short 8-day post-Peak phase, suggesting the entire cycle was hormonally abnormal.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "40-day cycle...prolonged mucus buildup that concludes on day 32...very short post-Peak phase numbering only eight days" Section: Figure 28-4.

facts.claim: Stress-induced cycle aberrations must be differentiated from cervical inflammation, but stressed cycles do not meet the diagnostic criteria for cervical inflammation (see Chapter 25).
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Neither of these two cycles meets the criteria for a cervical inflammation (see Chapter 25)." Section: Figure 28-4.

facts.claim: One proposed physiological mechanism for stress-induced ovulatory dysfunction involves β-endorphins blocking the normal pulsatile production of FSH and LH from the pituitary.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "β-endorphins play a role in blocking the normal, pulsatile production of FSH and LH from the pituitary." Section: Physiological Mechanisms.

facts.claim: Physical stressors that affect the cycle include sickness, strenuous activity, and travel.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Examples of physical stress include such things as sickness, strenuous activity and travel." Section: Overview.

facts.claim: The 'double' Peak phenomenon from stress may represent a protective mechanism that delays ovulation until the stressor is relieved.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "It is a situation that can happen to any woman; it may be a protective mechanism at work." Section: Overview.

facts.claim: Strenuous exercise combined with weight loss (dieting) can convert normal mucus cycles into infertile dry cycles, representing a stress-induced functional infertility pattern.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Because of the dry cycle pattern, she converted her cycles to infertile cycles." Section: Figure 28-3.

facts.claim: Charting with the CREIGHTON MODEL FertilityCare System (CrMS) provides women with self-knowledge that enables lifestyle evaluation to identify and reduce stress-related cycle effects.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "With the self-knowledge that comes with charting the CREIGHTON MODEL FertilityCare™ System (CrMS), women may be able to take a look at their lifestyle" Section: Self-Knowledge.

facts.claim: Emotional stressors affecting the cycle include change of job, bereavement, major decisions, holidays, relatives visiting, weddings, and exams.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Examples of emotional stress could be change of job, bereavement, major decisions, holidays, relatives visiting, weddings and exams." Section: Overview.

facts.claim: A classic charting aberration due to stress is the 'double' Peak, where stress delays ovulation and a second Peak occurs after the stress is relieved. Ovulation occurs with the second Peak.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The classic example a charting aberration due to stress is the 'double' Peak. This is a situation in which stress delays ovulation" Section: Overview.

facts.claim: Stress-induced charting effects include prolonged Peak-type mucus buildup, delayed Peak Day and ovulation, conversion to completely dry cycles, or conversion to limited mucus cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "prolonged Peak-type mucus buildup while others may have a delay in the Peak Day and ovulation. In others, the charting pattern may turn totally dry" Section: Overview.

facts.claim: In a case example (Figure 28-1), a minor surgical procedure on day 11 delayed the Peak Day to day 20 (9 days after surgery), extending cycle length to 34 days.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "minor surgical procedure on day 11 of her third cycle of charting has a Peak Day occurring on day 20 of the cycle or nine days after the surgery" Section: Case Example Figure 28-1.

facts.claim: In a bereavement case (Figure 28-2), a grandfather's death on day 15 and funeral on day 22 delayed Peak Day to day 27, with a 20-day mucus cycle and 42-day overall cycle length.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "her grandfather died (on day 15)...funeral was delayed until day 22...Peak Day did not occur until day 27" Section: Figure 28-2.

facts.claim: Following the bereavement case in Figure 28-2, a normal 15-day post-Peak phase occurred despite overall cycle prolongation to 42 days.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This was followed by a normal 15-day, post-Peak phase but the cycle length itself was prolonged at 42 days in duration." Section: Figure 28-2.

facts.claim: In the bereavement case, recovery from the stress-induced cycle aberration took approximately two cycles before ovulation returned to normal.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "it took about two cycles for her ovulation cycles to recuperate from the stressful event." Section: Figure 28-2.

facts.claim: Some premenstrual symptoms occur in approximately 75 percent of women, while moderate to severe premenstrual symptoms that disrupt lifestyle occur in 20 to 30 percent, and severe debilitating symptoms in 2 to 10 percent.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Menorrhagia: Heavier than normal bleeding. Metrorrhagia: Bleeding between menstrual periods." Section: Medical Definitions.

facts.claim: Hilgers distinguishes three fibroid types (submucous, intramural, subserous) and identifies submucous fibroids as the most problematic for bleeding; even small submucous fibroids can cause extremely heavy flow.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A small submucous fibroid can cause extremely heavy menstrual flow." Section: Excessively Heavy Menses.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In one cycle, it totaled nearly 500 cc. She had a decrease in her hemoglobin with each of her menstrual periods" Section: Figure 32-11.

facts.claim: Of four histologic features evaluated in endometrial hyperplasia (stromal invasion, mitotic activity, nuclear atypia, stratification), stromal invasion is the most reliable indicator of invasive carcinoma.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Of these four, stromal invasion is the most reliable indicator of invasive carcinoma." Section: Endometrial Hyperplasia.

facts.claim: Hysteroscopy is the optimal method of assessing endometrial hyperplasia because the entire endometrial surface can be visualized; blind endometrial biopsy cannot sample all of the endometrium.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Hysteroscopy is the optimal method of assessing endometrial hyperplasia because the entire surface of the endometrium can be visualized." Section: Endometrial Hyperplasia.

facts.claim: CrMS defines excessively heavy menses as at least one 24-48 hour period where the woman must change pads, tampons or both more frequently than every two hours.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Pope Paul VI Institute research found that in abnormal cycles, the average duration of tail-end brown bleeding was 2.2 days.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Approximately 10 million Americans have osteoporosis, and nearly 34 million (55 percent of those aged 50 and older) have low bone mass that increases their risk of developing osteoporosis and related fractures.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "10 million individuals have the disease, and nearly 34 million (or 55 percent of those 50 years of age and older) have a low bone mass" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Dual-energy x-ray absorptiometry (DXA) is the preferred method for diagnosing decreased bone mineral density; it uses two x-ray beams of different energy levels to correct for beam path length and tissue composition.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The preferred method for diagnosing decreased BMD is dual-energy x-ray absorptiometry (DXA)." Section: Diagnosis of Osteoporosis.

facts.claim: BMD measurements obtained by DXA at peripheral sites are most predictive of fracture risk in women older than 65.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "BMD measurements obtained by DXA at peripheral sites are most predictive for fracture risk in women who are older than 65." Section: Diagnosis.

facts.claim: A T-score expresses BMD as standard deviations above (+) or below (-) the mean for young, normal adults of the same sex, and is used as the convenient cut-point for osteoporosis treatment decisions.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "expressed as a standard deviation (SD) above (+) or below (-) the mean. T-scores... are convenient cut-points for treatment decisions." Section: Diagnosis.

facts.claim: Pharmacologic agents used to prevent and treat osteoporosis include alendronate (Fosamax), calcitonin-salmon nasal spray (Miacalcin), and raloxifene (Evista).

source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.claim: Prior (1990) hypothesized that progesterone is a bone trophic hormone active in bone metabolism, with the bone remodeling unit beginning a formation phase when progesterone levels peak during the mid-luteal phase.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Prior has suggested that progesterone may be a bone trophic hormone... progesterone was active in bone metabolism." Section: Hormonal Correlations.

facts.claim: In Pope Paul VI Institute research, a decreased post-Peak estradiol-17β (E₂) sum was associated with significantly lower bone densities in the spine and the femoral neck in reproductive-age women.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Patients with decreased post-Peak E₂ sums had significantly lower bone densities in the spine and the femoral neck" Section: Pope Paul VI Institute Research.

facts.claim: A Type IV luteal phase defect is defined by hormonal dysfunction uniquely E₂ in origin, with normal periovulatory E₂ and post-Peak progesterone but significantly reduced luteal phase E₂ levels.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.claim: In reproductive-age women with CrMS mucus cycle scores ≤5.3, hip BMD was significantly lower (p=.015 total hip; p=.002) and spine BMD was near-significant (p=.055) compared to women with higher mucus cycle scores.

source.journal: Testosterone Deficiency in Men

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When the menstrual score was less than or equal to 5.1, the BMD for the femoral neck and total hip were statistically lower" Section: Pope Paul VI Institute Research.

facts.claim: Low bone mass prior to menopause is associated with early postmenopausal fractures, suggesting that identifying at-risk premenopausal women offers opportunity for early therapy during reproductive years.

facts.claim: Potentially modifiable osteoporosis risk factors include current cigarette smoking, low body weight (less than 127 pounds), estrogen deficiency (early menopause before age 45, bilateral oophorectomy, or premenopausal amenorrhea >1 year), heavy alcohol intake, inadequate physical activity, and low calcium intake.

facts.claim: In 82 TSD patients treated with SRT3, dosages ranged from 22.5 to 82.5 µg every 12 hours with an average dose of 45.4 µg every 12 hours.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The dosages range from 22.5 to 82.5 µg every (Q) 12 hours. The average dose of 45.4 µg every 12 hours." Section: Results of Treatment.

facts.claim: Patients on SRT3 showed statistically significant pulse increases: 7-10 bpm during pre-Peak phase and 8-9 bpm during postovulatory phase.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "pulse rate increased by 7 to 10 beats per minute during the pre-Peak phase of the cycle and by 8 to 9 beats per minute during the postovulatory phase" Section: Results.

facts.claim: During SRT3 therapy, total T4, free T4, TSH, and rT3 all decreased significantly while total T3 increased, producing a significant increase in the T3/rT3 ratio.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The total T₄, free T₄, TSH, and rT₃ all decreased while the total T₃ increased. As a result, the T₃/rT₃ ratio also increased" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Additional SRT3 side effects include flu-like feeling/achiness (36.6%) and fluid retention/puffiness (25.6%); many are transient as patients accommodate to the medication.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "flu-like feeling/achiness (36.6%); and fluid retention/puffiness (25.6%). Many of these symptoms were only transient" Section: Results.

facts.claim: In the Mackowiak JAMA 1992 study (N=148), mean body temperature for women (n=26) was 98.4°F versus 98.1°F for men (n=122), supporting Wilson's 98.6°F reference for female patients.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the average body temperature for the men in this study (n=122) was 98.1 degrees Fahrenheit. The average temperature for women was 98.4" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: If heart rate exceeds 100 bpm on SRT3, protocol is to reduce the dose or add propranolol 10 mg PO BID or TID to control symptoms.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If the heart rate gets above 100 beats per minute, we have advised that the patients either decrease their dosage or...add propranolol 10 mg" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Per Wilson's original description, SRT3 maintenance therapy for 30-60 days can resolve TSD such that body temperature remains normal after tapering.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "after 30 to 60 days of maintenance therapy on the SRT₃, the condition could be 'cured' and the body temperature would stay up" Section: Diagnosis.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "associated with an increased number of pregnancies in the infertility program and an improvement in the treatment of premenstrual symptoms" Section: Final Note.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a positive effect on the subsequent miscarriage rate is being reported in this textbook and is found in Chapter 57" Section: Final Note.

facts.claim: The 2005 American Thyroid Association statement concluded that biomedical literature found no scientific evidence supporting Wilson's syndrome; Hilgers disputes this based on Mackowiak temperature data re-analysis.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "thorough review of the biomedical literature has found no scientific evidence supporting the existence of Wilson's syndrome" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: In TSD, chronic stress elevates cortisol which inhibits T4-to-T3 conversion and favors T4-to-reverse-T3 (rT3) conversion, producing reverse T3 dominance that persists after cortisol levels normalize.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Cortisol can inhibit the conversion of thyroxine (T₄) to triiodothyronine (T₃). Cortisol favors the conversion of T₄ to reverse T₃" Section: Theory.

facts.claim: Reverse T3 is chemically similar to T3 but has no physiologic activity; both T3 and rT3 bind the same cellular receptors, so excess rT3 blocks T3 activity and produces hypometabolism.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Reverse T₃ is chemically similar to T₃ but is completely inactive (it has no physiologic activity)" Section: Theory.

facts.claim: Pope Paul VI Institute TSD diagnosis protocol requires oral body temperature measurement on cycle days 5-9 and Peak +5 through Peak +9, capturing follicular and luteal phase temperatures.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "measurement of body temperature (oral) at day 5 through 9 and Peak + 5 through 9 of the menstrual cycle" Section: Diagnosis.

facts.claim: TSD temperature protocol requires measuring temperature upon awakening plus at 11:00 a.m., 2:00 p.m., and 5:00 p.m.; the last three daytime readings are averaged for the daily average.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the temperature is measured upon awakening and during the day at 11:00 a.m., 2:00 p.m., and 5:00 p.m." Section: Diagnosis.

facts.claim: TSD is diagnosed when body temperature is below 98.2°F, symptoms are positive on the A-Z Symptom Check List, and the T3/rT3 ratio is less than 10.0.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If the body temperature is below 98.2 degrees Fahrenheit, the list of symptoms is positive and the T₃/rT₃ ratio is less than 10.0" Section: Diagnosis.

facts.claim: TSD laboratory workup includes total T4, free T4, TSH, total T3, and reverse T3 levels.

source.title: Chapter 36: Thyroid System Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "levels of total T₄, free T₄, TSH, total T₃, and reverse T₃ are drawn" Section: Diagnosis.

source.journal: A Complete Textbook for GNM Internship

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The incidence of polycystic ovaries (PCO) has been observed to be 14.2 percent in a group of otherwise healthy women." Section: Introduction.

source.journal: A Complete Textbook for GNM Internship

facts.claim: Metformin directly inhibits androgen production at the human theca cell and has direct effects on ovarian steroidogenesis.

source.title: Chapter-02 Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome: A Metabolic Disease

source.title: Chapter-02 Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome: A Metabolic Disease
source.journal: A Complete Textbook for GNM Internship

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the use of metformin therapy during the course of pregnancy will reduce the development of gestational diabetes in these patients." Section: Insulin Resistance.

facts.claim: Non-classic adrenal hyperplasia (NCAH), diagnosed by elevated 17-hydroxyprogesterone, affects 1-10 percent of hyperandrogenemic women; 40 percent of NCAH patients also have polycystic ovaries.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "40 percent of patients with NCAH will also demonstrate evidence of polycystic ovaries." Section: 21-Hydroxylase Deficiency.

facts.claim: On Creighton Model charting, PCOD patients show average cycle length of 44.6 days, post-Peak phase average 15 days, and mucus cycle score of 4.82 (significantly decreased, p<.001).

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The average cycle length is 44.6 days, the average post-Peak phase is 15 days (but it can be highly variable) and the mucus cycle score is 4.82." Section: CrMS in PCOD.

facts.claim: Cycles 32-38 days in duration on CrMS charting are often associated with polycystic ovaries; cycles >38 days are the classic hallmark of PCOD.

source.title: Chapter-02 Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome: A Metabolic Disease
source.journal: A Complete Textbook for GNM Internship

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Cycles that are 32 to 38 days in duration are often associated with PCO as well" Section: CrMS in PCOD.

facts.claim: Progesterone prophylaxis protocol at Pope Paul VI Institute: IM progesterone 100 mg on days 18, 21, and 24 of the cycle to protect endometrium from unopposed estrogen.

source.journal: A Complete Textbook for GNM Internship

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the progesterone can be given in a dosage of 100 mg on Peak +4, 7, and 10." Section: Progesterone Prophylaxis.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Premature menopause presents with cessation of periods, hot flashes, and highly elevated FSH and LH levels on hormonal evaluation.

source.title: Chapter 45: Amenorrhea and Anovulation
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The periods stop, the woman begins to have hot flashes and upon evaluation hormonally, her FSH and LH levels are highly elevated." Section: Premature menopause.

source.title: Chapter 45: Amenorrhea and Anovulation
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "due to a waxing and waning of tonic levels of estrogen which are enough to stimulate the cervix and produce a mucus discharge." Section: Charting patterns.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "anorexia nervosa, malnutrition, Cushing syndrome, and gonadal dysgenesis. In some rare cases, the anovulatory pattern may have an autoimmune etiology." Section: Other conditions.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.title: Chapter 45: Amenorrhea and Anovulation
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the most common cyclic pattern observed is one of long and irregular cycles. The actual occurrence of amenorrhea is not common" Section: PCOS paragraph.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In hypothalamic amenorrhea, the standard amenorrhea profile (Table 45-1) will be normal." Section: Hypothalamic amenorrhea.

source.title: Chapter 45: Amenorrhea and Anovulation
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.title: Chapter 45: Amenorrhea and Anovulation
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a precondition towards the development of osteoporosis because of the hypoestrogenic state." Section: Hypothalamic amenorrhea.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "bromocriptine (Parlodel), which will suppress the production of prolactin and help shrink the tumor, or a newer medication called Dostinex" Section: Hyperprolactinemia.

facts.claim: NaProTECHNOLOGY treats infertile women with regular menstrual cycles using a comprehensive Ovarian and Target Organ Dysfunction (OTOD) framework, addressing cervix, endometrium, ovary, myometrium, and fallopian tube rather than ovulation induction alone.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This ovarian and target organ dysfunction (OTOD) becomes a major, multi-factorial focus of a NaProTECHNOLOGY approach to treatment." Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Menotropins (HMG) are supplied as lyophilized powder containing 75 IU of FSH and 75 IU of LH per ampule, extracted from postmenopausal urine.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "supplied as lyophilized powder which contains 75 IU of FSH and 75 IU of LH per ampule." Section: Human Menopausal Gonadotropins.

facts.claim: NaProTECHNOLOGY targets a preovulatory E₂ Peak level between 26.0 ng/dL and 90.0 ng/dL on low-dose menotropin therapy, measured every other day from Peak -6 through Peak +2.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: HCG is approximately six times more potent than LH because of its prolonged half-life, providing more stable stimulation of LH receptors over 24-hour intervals.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "because its half-life is more prolonged, HCG is approximately six times more potent than LH." Section: Use of HCG.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: The NaPro high-dose clomiphene progression for PCOS starts at 50 mg days 5-9, escalating by 50 mg if no period by day 35 after IM progesterone 200 mg induction, up to 150 mg PO QD days 5-9.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If no menstrual period occurs, and/or no pregnancy occurs over a six-cycle time frame ... the patient is considered to be clomiphene resistant." Section: High-dose Clomiphene Program.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the best pregnancy rates ultimately come as a result of ovarian wedge resection." Section: High-dose Clomiphene Program.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Pulsatile GnRH delivers a preset intravenous dose every 90 minutes via computerized pump; Lutrepulse (Ayerst, 100 mcg vials) is used for hypothalamic amenorrhea and Kallmann syndrome.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "administration of GnRH by discrete pulses every 90 minutes could restore normal gonadotropin release." Section: Gonadotropin-releasing Hormone.

facts.claim: Type V luteal phase deficiency (isolated E₂ deficiency) is supplemented with 1-2 mg E₂ PO QD hs or 5 mg triestrogen PO QD hs from Peak +3 through Peak +12, or vaginally in non-sustained-release form.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "1 mg to 2 mg by mouth every day at bedtime (PO QD hs) Peak +3 through 12" Section: Luteal Phase Deficiency.

facts.claim: Check et al. reported that for LUF syndrome the best treatment is a combination of HMG plus a triggering dose of HCG or a combination of HCG and HMG.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The very best treatment for the LUF syndrome was with a combination of HMG and a triggering dose of either HCG or a combination of HCG and HMG." Section: For Ovulation Defects.

facts.claim: Vitamin B₆ 500 mg in sustained-release form taken daily improves cervical mucus production; in one case mucus cycle score (MCS) increased from 1.0 to 5.7 with B₆ supplementation.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In the absence of Vitamin B₆, the MCS was 1.0. With the addition of Vitamin B₆, it increased to 5.7." Section: Mucus Enhancers.

facts.claim: Ampicillin (a penicillin antibiotic) is the most effective cervical mucus enhancer: in one patient MCS rose from 0.0 (no antibiotic) to 8.0 with ampicillin and returned to 1.0 when discontinued.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In the second cycle, ampicillin was used as a mucus enhancer and the mucus cycle score increased to 8.0" Section: Mucus Enhancers.

facts.claim: NaPro mucus-enhancing agents include amoxicillin, ampicillin, cefaclor (Ceclor), pyridoxine (Vitamin B₆), guaifenesin, and prednisone.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Mucus-enhancing agents include antibiotics such as amoxicillin, ampicillin or cefaclor (Ceclor), vitamin B₆ (pyridoxine hydrochloride), guaifenesin" Section: Implementation of Protocols.

facts.claim: Approximately 10% of clomiphene-treated women experience vasomotor flushes or hot flashes; visual disturbances occur in under 2% and warrant discontinuation.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "About 10 percent of clomiphene-treated women will experience vasomotor flushes or hot flashes." Section: Side Effects - Clomiphene Citrate.

facts.claim: The multiple-gestation rate from clomiphene is approximately 8% overall, but has been reduced to 3.2% at the Pope Paul VI Institute using low-dose protocols.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the rates at the Pope Paul VI Institute, the overall multiple gestation rate has been reduced to 3.2 percent." Section: Side Effects - Clomiphene Citrate.

facts.claim: Clomiphene citrate was synthesized in 1956, first clinical trials 1960, first published successful human ovulation induction 1961, and FDA approval for clinical use 1967.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Clomiphene citrate (Clomid; Serophene) was synthesized in 1956 and was first introduced for clinical trials as early as 1960." Section: Clomiphene Citrate.

facts.claim: Multi-fetal gestation rates with superovulation gonadotropin protocols range from 20% to 45%, driven by targeting 8-10 follicles per ovary.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The multi-fetal gestation rate can be very high ranging from 20 to 45 percent." Section: Side Effects - Gonadotropins.

facts.claim: Pyridoxine (B₆) sensory neuropathy has been reported at doses of 200-5,000 mg/day (nearly all cases >2 g/day); over 25 years at Pope Paul VI Institute only one case of mild sensory tingling occurred on 500 mg sustained-release.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The pyridoxine dose was 200 to 5,000 mg per day, and in almost all cases, the dosage was beyond two gram per day." Section: Side Effects - Vitamin B₆.

facts.claim: Persistent unruptured follicles (luteal cysts) are a contraindication to HCG use; painful unruptured follicles are best treated with IM progesterone 100-200 mg to aid dissolution.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "give, intramuscularly, progesterone 100 or 200 mg. This will decrease the pain within a short period of time after its administration" Section: Side Effects - HCG.

facts.claim: Letrozole (Femara) for ovulation induction is dosed 2.5 mg QD days 3-7, or as a single 20 mg dose on day 3 of the cycle with similar ovarian stimulation outcomes.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Letrozole (Femara) has been used at a dosage of 2.5 mg every day (QD) from days 3 through 7." Section: Other Medications For Treating Ovarian Dysfunction.

facts.claim: Anastrozole produces >99.1% average total body aromatase inhibition versus 97.3% with letrozole (as reported in-chapter, though chapter labels both figures to anastrozole).

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The average percentage of total body aromatase inhibition with anastrozole is greater than 99.1 percent and with anastrozole it is 97.3 percent." Section: Other Medications.

facts.claim: Urinary FSH biologic activity is 700 IU/gram versus pituitary FSH at 14,000 IU/gram, but clinical effects are indistinguishable at comparable doses of FSH.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The biologic activity of urinary FSH is 700 IU/gram compared with 14,000 IU/gram of pituitary FSH." Section: Human Menopausal Gonadotropins.

facts.claim: GnRH was first isolated, sequenced, and synthesized by Schally and Guillemin in 1971; first pulsed-GnRH pregnancies in anovulatory women were reported in 1980.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) was first isolated, sequenced and synthesized by Schally and Guillemin in 1971." Section: Gonadotropin-releasing Hormone.

facts.claim: Abnormal bleeding (premenstrual spotting, tail-end brown bleeding) on the CrMS chart may reflect decreased progesterone production or chronic endometritis; first-line antibiotics are clarithromycin or metronidazole.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The recommended antibiotic would be either clarithromycin or metronidazole." Section: Abnormal Bleeding and Discharge.

facts.claim: Continuous mucus discharge consistent with cervical eversion is treated by hyfercation of the cervix in NaProTECHNOLOGY.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If a continuous mucus discharge occurs which is consistent with the presence of a cervical eversion, then the cervix should be hyfercated." Section: Abnormal Bleeding and Discharge.

facts.claim: Clomiphene citrate is a racemic mixture of cis-isomer (zuclomiphene, the active component) and trans-isomer (enclomiphene), typically 38% zuclomiphene and 62% enclomiphene.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The racemic mixture is generally made up of 38 percent enclomiphene citrate and 62 percent of enclomiphene citrate." Section: Clomiphene Citrate.

facts.claim: Recombinant HCG (r-HCG) 250 µg single dose is at least as effective as 5,000-10,000 IU u-HCG, with fewer injection-site adverse reactions.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A single dose of r-HCG 250 µg was found to be at least as effective as a single dose of 5,000 to 10,000 IU of u-HCG" Section: Other Medications.

facts.claim: OHSS management requires maintaining hematocrit <45% to avoid hemoconcentration, with hospitalization for fluid balance, vital organ perfusion, hypercoagulation prevention, and pain control.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Hemoconcentration should be avoided, maintaining the hematocrit at less than 45 percent." Section: Side Effects - Gonadotropins.

facts.claim: Clomiphene citrate occupies nuclear estrogen receptor proteins for weeks, in contrast to native estrogens which clear the cell within 24 hours.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "clomiphene citrate has been shown to occupy nuclear receptor proteins for weeks on end" Section: Clomiphene Citrate.

facts.claim: The NaPro low-dose clomiphene progression for infertile women with regular cycles starts at 25 mg (1/2 tablet) PO daily on days 3-5 of the cycle, escalating to 50 mg days 3-5, then 50 mg days 5-7 if needed.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the starting dose is 25 mg of clomiphene (1/2 tablet) by mouth daily, from day 3 through 5 of the cycle." Section: Low-dose Clomiphene Progression.

facts.claim: NaProTECHNOLOGY clomiphene monitoring targets a Peak +7 progesterone level of 13-15 ng/mL or greater and a Peak +7 E₂ level of 12.0 ng/dL or greater.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The aim is to achieve a progesterone level that is 13-15 ng/mL or greater and an E₂ level which is 12.0 ng/dL or greater." Section: Low-dose Clomiphene Progression.

facts.claim: The low-dose menotropin (HMG) progression for infertile women with regular cycles starts at 75 IU IM on cycle days 3-7, escalating to 150 IU days 3-7 if inadequate response, with strict E₂ and ultrasound monitoring.

source.title: Chapter 46: Medical Treatment of Ovarian Dysfunction
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The program starts with one ampule (75 IU) given intramuscularly from days 3 to 7 of the cycle." Section: Low-dose Menotropin Progression.

facts.claim: The Pope Paul VI Institute defines Effective Sperm Count (ESC) as count/cc multiplied by percent motility multiplied by percent normal morphology.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "ESC (the count/cc x percent motility x percent normals)." Section: Classification of oligospermia.

facts.claim: Overall completed pregnancy rate with ICSI ranges only from 15 to 19 percent.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The overall completed pregnancy rate in patients being treated with ICSI ranges only from 15 to 19 percent." Section: ICSI.

facts.claim: Approximately 75 percent of patients with oligospermia can expect an improvement in sperm count when treated with clomiphene citrate.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Approximately 75 percent of patients with oligospermia can expect an improvement in their sperm count with the use of clomiphene." Section: Medical Treatment.

facts.claim: Standard clomiphene dose for oligospermia is 25 mg per day for 25 days followed by a 5-6 day rest period.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The most recommended dosage of clomiphene is 25 mg per day for 25 days followed by a rest period of five or six days" Section: Medical Treatment.

facts.claim: Pope Paul VI Institute's empirical clomiphene dose for oligospermia is 5 mg by mouth twice daily, prepared through a compounding pharmacy.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Our standard dose of clomiphene in oligospermia is 5 mg by mouth two times a day (PO BID). This can be prepared through a compounding pharmacy." Section: Medical Treatment.

facts.claim: Intramuscular HCG has a circulating half-life of approximately 30 to 40 hours and significantly increases plasma testosterone 24 hours after administration.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "HCG has a long circulating half-life (about 30 to 40 hours), significantly increasing plasma testosterone levels 24 hours after HCG application." Section: Other Treatments.

facts.claim: Clonidine 0.075 mg orally at bedtime can improve spermatogenesis in oligospermic patients with maturation arrest over a 2-3 month period.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A dosage of 0.075 mg is given orally at bedtime... oligospermic patients responded over a two to three month period" Section: Other Treatments.

facts.claim: Raw egg white is the only vaginal lubricant not shown to be deleterious to sperm, but it is not recommended due to foreign protein allergic reaction risk.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Raw egg white is the only one that has been shown not to be deleterious to the sperm... the potential to have a foreign protein allergic reaction" Section: Vaginal Lubricants.

facts.claim: K-Y Jelly and Surgilube are especially spermicidal; petroleum jelly and glycerine were found to have the least detrimental effects on sperm motility among 15 lubricants tested.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "petroleum jelly and glycerine have had the least detrimental affects on motility... K-Y® Jelly or Surgilube® are especially spermicidal." Section: Vaginal Lubricants.

facts.claim: CrMS fertility-focused intercourse successfully achieved pregnancy in a case with effective sperm count of only 30,000/cc, demonstrating severe oligospermia can be overcome with accurate timing.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In patient A, the effective sperm count was 30,000/cc... pregnancies in severe oligospermia" Section: CREIGHTON MODEL FertilityCare System.

facts.claim: In a Pope Paul VI Institute review of 538 male infertility patients, 53.0% were normospermic, 45.9% were oligospermic, and 1.1% were azoospermic.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "261 (53.0%) of the men were normospermic. On the other hand, 45.9 percent were oligospermic and 1.1 percent were azoospermic." Section: Male Status Evaluation.

facts.claim: In one CrMS severe oligospermia pregnancy case, Peak Day occurred on day 30 with a single 'tacky clear' (8K x 1) mucus observation, which was the only peak-type observation in the entire cycle.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Peak Day occurred on day 30 of the cycle with an observation of 'tacky clear' (8K x 1). This was the only peak mucus observation of the entire cycle." Section: CrMS case studies.

facts.claim: In a CrMS severe oligospermia pregnancy case, ultrasound-estimated due date and Peak Day-estimated due date were identical within one day.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The due date as estimated by ultrasound and the due date estimated by the Peak Day were identical within one day." Section: CrMS case studies.

facts.claim: Vitamin E oral administration significantly improved in vitro function of human spermatozoa as an antioxidant against reactive oxygen species in male infertility.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "An oral administration of vitamin E significantly improved the in vitro function of human spermatozoa." Section: Other Treatments.

facts.claim: HCG dosage forms for male infertility range from 2,500 IU twice weekly over 6-8 weeks to higher protocols, improving both sperm motility and pregnancy rates.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "2,500 IU given twice weekly over a period of six to eight weeks... This has also improved pregnancy rates." Section: Other Treatments.

facts.claim: Pope Paul VI Institute standard parameters consider a semen analysis normal if count >20 million/cc, motility >60%, and morphology >60% normal forms.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "if the count is greater than 20 million, the motility is greater than 60 percent and the morphology reflects greater than 60 percent normal sperm" Section: Semen Analysis Minimal Standards.

facts.claim: WHO reference values for normal semen differ from Pope Paul VI: sperm concentration >20M/cc, motility >50%, and strict-Kruger morphology >15% normal.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "motility is considered normal if it is greater than 50 percent and the morphology is normal if greater than 15 percent." Section: WHO Reference Criteria.

facts.claim: Intrauterine insemination in natural cycles for male factor infertility yields pregnancy rates ranging from 0.0 to 9.0 percent.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When IUI is used in natural cycles (Table 47-6), the pregnancy rate ranges from 0.0 to 9.0 percent." Section: Treatment IUI and ICSI.

facts.claim: IUI in clomiphene citrate-stimulated cycles for male factor infertility yields pregnancy rates ranging from 0.1 to 16.0 percent.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the pregnancy rates range from 0.1 to 16.0 percent (Table 47-7)" Section: IUI in clomiphene-stimulated cycles.

facts.claim: IUI in HMG-stimulated cycles for male factor infertility yields pregnancy rates ranging from 2.0 to 15 percent.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "with HMG-stimulated cycles the pregnancy rate ranges from 2.0 to 15 percent (Table 47-8)." Section: IUI HMG-stimulated.

facts.claim: NaProTechnology consultations last an average of 40 to 45 minutes, longer than most other specialties, with couples reviewed every three to four months.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "An average consultation will last between 40 to 45 minutes." Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Seminal fluid is collected during intercourse using a perforated non-spermicidal plastic seminal fluid collecting device to capture the full sample including pre-ejaculatory fluid.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "wearing a perforated non-spermicidal plastic seminal fluid collecting device. This allows for the full sample to be collected." Section: Male Fertility.

facts.claim: The two main new female factors NPT identifies beyond standard evaluation are cervical mucus quality abnormalities and suboptimal luteal-phase progesterone/estradiol 17β levels.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "woman's cervical mucus quality or suboptimal levels of progesterone and estradiol 17ß in the luteal phase" Section: Female Fertility.

facts.claim: The husband records the most fertile observation on the CrMS chart each day after the woman observes mucus markers throughout the day.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "her husband records the most fertile observation on the chart at the end of each day." Section: Female Fertility.

facts.claim: An effective cycle requires normal P+7 progesterone/estradiol, satisfactory mucus scores, frequent intercourse on fertile days, and minimized stress/tiredness.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Peak +7 blood results for progesterone and estradiol are in the normal range - Cervical mucus scores are satisfactory" Section: Overview of Treatment Course.

facts.claim: Achieving 12 effective cycles may require 15 to 18 total cycles, since cycles only count when effective cycle conditions are met.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "It may take 15 to 18 total cycles to achieve 12 effective ones." Section: Overview of Treatment Course.

facts.claim: PMS lasting four or more days is commonly associated with progesterone deficiency; two to three days of premenstrual symptoms is considered normal.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Premenstrual syndrome (PMS) lasting for four or more days is commonly associated with progesterone deficiency." Section: Symptoms.

facts.claim: Day 3 baseline blood tests in NPT include FSH, LH, prolactin, thyroid function, vitamin B12, rubella, complete blood count, and testosterone if indicated.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Blood tests are taken on day 3 of the cycle for FSH, LH, prolactin, thyroid function, vitamin B12, rubella, complete blood count and testosterone" Section: Investigations - Level 1.

facts.claim: Full NPT hormone profile samples estradiol 17-β on Peak -3, -1, +1 and both estradiol and progesterone on Peak +3, +5, +7, +9, and +11.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "estradiol 17-β pre-ovulation on Peak -3, -1, +1 and a combination of estradiol 17-β (E₂) and progesterone on Peak + 3, 5, 7, 9 and 11." Section: Investigations - Level 1.

facts.claim: Normal Peak Plus progesterone reference range (Ireland) is 18.5-31.0 ng/mL (60-100 nmol/L) and normal Peak Plus estradiol is 145-300 pg/mL (400-800 pmol/L).
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Progesterone (18.5 - 31.0 ng/mL) (60-100 nmol/L) Estradiol (145-300 pg/mL) (400-800 pmol/L)" Section: Normal Ranges.

facts.claim: Normal pre-Peak estradiol (P-3, P-1, P+1) is over 370 pg/mL (1000 nmol/L) in the NPT Irish reference range.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Pre Peak P-3, P-1, P+1 Estradiol only - Over 370 pg/mL (1000 nmol/L)" Section: Normal Ranges.

facts.claim: An Irish NPT practice treating 1,239 couples over four years (Feb 1998 - Jan 2002) reported an overall live birth rate of 25.2%, rising to 44.7% for those who completed the program.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "yielded an overall live birth rate of 25.2 per cent, rising to 44.7 percent for those who successfully completed the program." Section: The Consultation Process.

facts.claim: Clomiphene dosing for NPT ovulation induction ranges from 25 mg daily for 3 days to 150 mg daily for 5 days starting Day 3, titrated until P+7 hormones reach normal.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "from 25 mg (one half tablet) daily for three days up to 150 mg (3 tablets) daily for five days starting on Day 3" Section: Investigations - Level 1.

facts.claim: HCG 5,000 or 10,000 units on P-1, Peak Day, or P+1 is used to facilitate follicular rupture when ultrasound shows incomplete rupture or luteinized unruptured follicle.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "HCG 5,000 or 10,000 units on P-1, Peak Day or P+1 can help to facilitate follicular rupture if incomplete rupture or LUF is found." Section: Investigations - Level 2.

facts.claim: Up to 50% of women experience improvement in cervical mucus quality with mucus-enhancing treatments including vitamin B6, mucolytics, and amoxicillin.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Up to 50 percent of women may get an improvement in mucus quality with these treatments." Section: Functional Abnormalities.

facts.claim: Clomiphene-resistant anovulation may respond to adding prednisolone 5 mg daily; failure rate is 1-2%, and <0.1% require pulsatile GnRH for hypothalamic amenorrhea.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "many women will ovulate if prednisolone 5 mg daily is added to the treatment. If this is not effective (1 to 2% of the time)" Section: Functional Abnormalities.

facts.claim: PCOD with increased insulin resistance is treated with metformin HCl 500 mg TID, titrated from half or one tablet daily; rosiglitazone 4 mg daily is an alternative.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "metformin HCl 500 mg three times a day (TID)... Rosiglitazone 4 mg daily is a useful alternative." Section: Functional Abnormalities.

facts.claim: Chronic low-grade endometritis causing persistent abnormal bleeding is treated with metronidazole 400 mg TID plus cephradine 500 mg TID for two weeks.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a two-week trial of metronidazole 400 mg three times a day (TID) and cephradine 500 mg three times a day is usually all it takes" Section: Functional Abnormalities.

facts.claim: PCOD ultrasound shows large ovaries with multiple small follicles measuring 0.5 to 0.7 mm in diameter; laparoscopy reveals pearly white ovaries.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Ultrasound shows large ovaries with multiple small follicles measuring 0.5 to 0.7 mm in diameter. A laparoscopy shows pearly white ovaries." Section: Functional Abnormalities.

facts.claim: Surgical evaluation is typically deferred until six effective cycles of medical treatment are complete, unless severe endo symptoms or female age >=38 without prior surgery.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Usually surgical assessment can wait until six effective cycles of medical treatment have been completed." Section: Investigations - Level 3.

facts.claim: In over 1,400 couples treated in general practice, no successful pregnancy was achieved for any female aged 45 years or greater.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "no successful pregnancy has been achieved for any females aged 45 years or greater." Section: The Consultation Process.

facts.claim: If significant abnormalities are found surgically during treatment, the 12-effective-cycle count is restarted after surgical correction.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If significant abnormalities are detected surgically, re-start count for 12 cycles after surgical correction." Section: Targeted Medical/Surgical Treatment.

facts.claim: Couples with a history of miscarriage are advised to avoid conception until monthly Peak +7 progesterone and estradiol reach the normal range to reduce miscarriage risk.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "advised to avoid conception until monthly Peak + 7 blood results for progesterone and estradiol have reached the normal range." Section: Overview of Treatment Course.

facts.claim: NPT PCOD diagnosis workup uses FSH:LH ratio reversal, elevated testosterone, insulin resistance markers, with ovarian biopsy histology able to confirm diagnosis.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Blood tests show a reversal of the FSH: LH ratio, elevated testosterone levels and decreased insulin resistance." Section: Functional Abnormalities.

facts.claim: The CREIGHTON MODEL FertilityCare System has been in use since 1978, establishing known normal chart patterns used to identify abnormal reproductive function.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "FertilityCare™ has been used by ever increasing numbers of couples since 1978" Section: Signs.

facts.claim: Approximately 1 in 6 couples today experience infertility requiring medical intervention.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "As many as one couple out of six today experience infertility problems which require medical intervention." Section: Signs.

facts.claim: First six NPT months are a lead-in: ~3 cycles to learn charting, cycle 2 hormone tests, treatment starts cycle 3-4, with ~3 more cycles to correct abnormalities.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The first six months are the lead in time before effective cycles of fertility treatment begin." Section: Overview of Treatment Course.

facts.claim: NPT abnormal chart signs include dry cycles, limited mucus, premenstrual spotting, intermenstrual spotting, tail-end brown bleeding, and short or long post-Peak phases.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Dry cycles - Limited mucus - Premenstrual spotting - Intermenstrual spotting - Tail-end brown bleeding - Short post-Peak phase" Section: Signs.

facts.claim: Male prednisolone immunosuppression is contraindicated in men over 50, with hypertension, diabetes, or previous TB exposure; screening includes BP, bloods, and chest x-ray.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "not recommended for men over 50 years old, those with high blood pressure, diabetes, or a previous exposure to TB." Section: Lifestyle Changes.

facts.claim: The IIRRM (International Institute of Restorative Reproductive Medicine) was formed in 2002 to promote non-destructive, non-circumventive research in reproductive medicine.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The International Institute of Restorative Reproductive Medicine (IIRRM) is an International research organization which was formed in 2002" Section: References.

facts.claim: For women attempting conception after 45 years of age, probability of conception is 1-3%, miscarriage risk exceeds 50%, and Down syndrome risk is 3%.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "low likelihood of conceiving (1% to 3%), a high probability of miscarriage (over 50%) or Down syndrome (3%) should conception occur." Section: The Consultation Process.

facts.claim: The full NPT treatment program lasts 18 to 24 months; couples who persist until completion have substantially higher success rates.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The entire program can last between 18 to 24 months before treatment would be discontinued and deemed unsuccessful." Section: The Consultation Process.

facts.claim: The sperm production cycle from sertoli cell to ejaculate takes 72 days, so repeat semen analysis is recommended three months after any intervention.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The cycle of sperm production from sertoli cell to ejaculate can take 72 days." Section: Lifestyle Changes.

facts.claim: Male infertility interventions produce moderate improvements in semen parameters in approximately 20-25% of men; sperm counts consistently below 1 million make conception less likely.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "usually improvements are moderate in about 20 to 25 percent of men. If the count remains consistently below one million" Section: Lifestyle Changes.

facts.claim: High-concentration anti-sperm antibodies are treated with prednisolone 20-40 mg BID for 10 days starting on day 1 or 5 of the wife's menstrual cycle.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "suppression of the male immune system with prednisolone 20 to 40 mg twice daily for 10 days beginning on day 1 or 5 of his wife's menstrual cycle." Section: Lifestyle Changes.

facts.claim: The CrMS follow-up schedule consists of five follow-ups during the first three months, followed by three additional follow-ups over the next nine months, totaling eight follow-ups in the first year.

source.title: Design and Long-Term Sustainability of Mini Health Centers for Primary Healthcare in Chennai, India.
