facts.claim: Up to one in five couples in the United States experience difficulty conceiving, and one in six couples in Europe experience infertility.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Up to one in five couples experience difficulty conceiving in the United States. In Europe one in six couples have difficulty conceiving" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Life-table analysis showed adjusted proportion achieving pregnancy was 26.2 percent at 12-17 months and 32.6 percent at 18-25 months in the NPT program.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "adjusted proportion achieving pregnancy is 26.2 percent at 12 to 17 months, rising to 32.6 percent for 18 to 25 months" Section: Results.

facts.claim: 58 percent (55/95) of failed-ART couples had an initial diagnosis of unexplained infertility prior to entering the NPT program.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "55 (58%) of the couples had an initial diagnosis, prior to coming into the NPT program, of unexplained infertility" Section: Analysis of 95 Patients.

facts.claim: After NPT evaluation none of the 95 couples retained a diagnosis of unexplained infertility; the category was eliminated via targeted hormone testing and CrMS charting.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "None of the couples had a diagnosis of unexplained infertility following NPT evaluation." Section: Analysis of 95 Patients.

facts.claim: HCG protocol after ovulation used 2000 units on days 3, 5, 7, and 9 post-Peak according to the FertilityCare chart; 48 of 95 women received this regimen.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "48 had a series of HCG injections of 2000 units on days 3, 5, 7 and 9 after ovulation according to the fertility chart" Section: Treatment summary.

facts.claim: Of the 95 failed-ART couples, 51 women received clomiphene ovulation induction and 49 received mucus-enhancing medications.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Fifty-one women had ovulation induction with clomiphene... Forty-nine had some form of mucus enhancing medications" Section: Treatment summary.

facts.claim: Fifteen women in the cohort conceived following surgical intervention: 10 after diathermy of endometriosis, 2 post-myomectomy, 1 post-polypectomy, and 2 after microsurgical tubal reconstruction.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "10 following diathermy of endometriosis, two post myomectomy, one post polypectomy and two had successful microsurgical reconstruction" Section: Treatment.

facts.claim: 84 progesterone-supported and 17 HCG-supported pregnancies received hormonal support to reduce miscarriage risk, typically for 2+ prior miscarriages or very low conception-cycle hormone levels.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "84 women had progesterone support and an additional 17 also had HCG in an attempt to reduce the risk of miscarriage" Section: Treatment.

facts.claim: Women aged 37 or younger who conceived had an 84 percent successful outcome; women aged 38+ had a 57.4 percent success rate with 42.6 percent adverse outcome risk.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "aged 37 years or less, 84 percent had a successful outcome... aged 38 years or greater, only 57.4 percent had a successful outcome" Section: Outcomes.

facts.claim: Overall miscarriage or ectopic pregnancy rate across all age groups in the failed-ART NPT cohort was 27.6 percent.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The overall risk of miscarriage or ectopic pregnancy for all age groups was 27.6 percent" Section: Outcomes.

facts.claim: Of women aged 42 or greater who conceived, 55 percent had an adverse pregnancy outcome.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Of women aged 42 years or greater, 55 percent had an adverse outcome to their pregnancy" Section: Outcomes.

facts.claim: Conventional infertility protocols assess ovulation via day 21 or 22 progesterone; most specialists agree ovulation is probably occurring if progesterone exceeds 30 nmol/L (9.5 ng/mL).
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "ovulation is probably occurring if progesterone levels exceed 30 nmol/L (9.5 ng/mL)" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Crude live birth rate with NPT declined as prior ART attempts increased: 28.9 percent after 1 prior ART, 18.4 percent after 2, and 13.3 percent after 3 or more.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "one previous attempt at ART the crude live birth rate was 28.9 percent. This fell to 18.4 percent... just 13.3 percent" Section: Outcomes.

facts.claim: Clinical consensus estimates approximately 5 percent of couples with failed IVF will conceive spontaneously without any further intervention.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "about 5 percent of couples with failed IVF will conceive subsequently without any intervention" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: Successful implantation/maintenance probability after NPT conception was 72.4 percent overall and 84 percent for women 37 or younger.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "probability of a successful maintenance of implantation was 72.4 percent for all age groups" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: In the UK in 1998-1999, 42 percent of couples attempting IVF had a diagnosis of unexplained infertility.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "42 percent of couples who attempted IVF in the United Kingdom in 1998-1999 had a diagnosis of unexplained infertility" Section: Conclusion.

facts.claim: Among failed-ART couples who conceived with NPT, 55 percent conceived beyond six months into the program and 10 required more than 20 months before conception.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "55 percent of couples who conceived beyond six months into the program and 10 couples who required more than 20 months" Section: Analysis.

facts.claim: Full NPT treatment course is designed to span 24 effective cycles, taking approximately 18 to 24 months from program start.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "complete the 24 effective cycles of treatment which can take from 18 to 24 months from beginning the program" Section: Background.

facts.claim: Among the 42 couples who had never conceived naturally before, 29 conceived through NPT though not all had live births, and 24 couples had a previous live birth.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "42 couples who had never conceived naturally before. Of these, 29 did conceive but never had a live birth" Section: Analysis.

facts.claim: One couple with hypothalamic amenorrhea achieved NPT pregnancy on the third cycle of pulsatile GnRH pump therapy.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "One couple with a diagnosis of hypothalamic amenorrhea required treatment using a GnRH pump. They conceived on their third cycle of pulsatile GnRH" Section: Treatment.

facts.claim: In a Galway, Ireland family practice, 20-30 percent of couples with previously unsuccessful ART achieved a successful pregnancy with NaProTECHNOLOGY.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "20 percent to 30 percent of couples with previously unsuccessful ART will have a successful pregnancy with NPT" Section: Background.

facts.claim: Seven men received fertility treatment in the cohort: 4 tamoxifen, 2 prednisolone, 1 antibiotics plus vitamin/mineral supplements.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Seven men required fertility treatment. Four had tamoxifen, two had prednisolone and one had antibiotics, vitamin and mineral supplements" Section: Treatment.

facts.claim: Women aged 34-39 years accounted for 64 percent (79/123) of conceptions; women 33 and younger only 15 percent and those 40+ accounted for 21 percent.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Women aged 33 years and younger account for only 15 percent (18/123) of conceptions, while those aged 40+ years account for 21 percent" Section: Analysis.

facts.claim: Among the 15 couples who conceived before completing investigations, 10 had a prior diagnosis of unexplained infertility that resolved simply through CrMS fertility tracking.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "15 couples who actually conceived before investigations were completed... 10 of the 15 had a previous diagnosis of unexplained infertility" Section: Analysis.

facts.claim: In the failed-ART cohort, 22 couples had endometriosis, 11 had abnormal seminal fluid analysis, 10 had anovulation, 10 had low progesterone, 8 had pelvic adhesions, and 7 had blocked tubes.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "22 couples had endometriosis, 10 had low progesterone, 10 were deemed not to be ovulating, 11 had an abnormal seminal fluid analysis" Section: Analysis.

facts.claim: The Boyle study enrolled 95 couples with previously failed ART between February 2, 1998 and February 1, 2004, producing 123 conceptions.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "There were 95 patients who conceived 123 times." Section: Study Group.

facts.claim: Average female age per conception in the failed-ART study cohort was 36.8 years, range 28-47 years.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The average age of the woman per conception was 36.8 years, with a range from 28 to 47 years" Section: Study Group.

facts.claim: Couples in the failed-ART NPT cohort had been trying to conceive an average of 6.1 years with 176 failed ART attempts and 175 unsuccessful embryo transfers.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "trying to conceive for an average of 6.1 years and had 176 failed attempts at ART, with 175 unsuccessful embryo transfers" Section: Study Group.

facts.claim: 95 couples achieved 123 conceptions, resulting in 89 successful pregnancies from 74 couples; 17 had a second successful pregnancy and 1 had a third.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "123 conceptions from 95 couples, resulting in 89 successful pregnancies from 74 of the couples" Section: Results with NPT Treatment.

facts.claim: Among the 123 NPT conceptions after failed ART, 17 ended in miscarriage and 4 in ectopic pregnancy.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "21 who did conceive, but either had a miscarriage (17) or ectopic pregnancy (4) as the final outcome" Section: Results with NPT Treatment.

facts.claim: The multiple pregnancy rate with NPT after failed ART was 3.4 percent per live birth (3 twin pregnancies) and 2.4 percent per conception.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "three twin pregnancies, giving a multiple pregnancy rate of 3.4 percent per live birth or 2.4 percent per conception" Section: Results.

facts.claim: The NaProTECHNOLOGY infertility study population analyzed in this chapter consisted of 1,045 patients treated for infertility.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "data will be presented in a variety of different ways on a total of 1,045 patients who have been treated for infertility by way of NaProTECHNOLOGY." Section: Results of NaProTECHNOLOGY.

facts.claim: In NaProTECHNOLOGY patients with tubal occlusion (including both proximal and distal), cumulative pregnancy rates exceeded 50 percent at 36 months.

source.title: Chapter 51: Effectiveness of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Cumulative pregnancy rates of over 50 percent were observed at 36 months." Section: Results of NaProTECHNOLOGY.

facts.claim: Among 11 NaProTECHNOLOGY patients with anovulation (1 Kallmann syndrome, 1 pituitary adenoma with hyperprolactinemia, 9 hypothalamic amenorrhea), 9 (81.8%) achieved pregnancy.

source.title: Chapter 51: Effectiveness of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Of these 11 patients, 9 succeeded in achieving pregnancy (81.8%)" Section: Results of NaProTECHNOLOGY.

facts.claim: In endometriosis patients treated with NaProTECHNOLOGY, cumulative pregnancy rates were statistically significantly lower in those with dry mucus cycles versus regular or limited mucus cycles.

source.title: Chapter 51: Effectiveness of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "there is... a statistically significant difference between the cumulative pregnancy rates in patients who have regular mucus cycles versus dry cycles" Section: Results.

facts.claim: NaProTECHNOLOGY patients with unruptured follicle ovulation defects (partial rupture or LUF) had a statistically significantly lower cumulative pregnancy rate than those with other ovulation patterns (P=.023).

source.title: Chapter 51: Effectiveness of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the group containing the unruptured follicle group has a statistically significantly lower cumulative pregnancy rate (P=.023)." Section: Results.

facts.claim: In NaProTECHNOLOGY infertility patients, presence or absence of cumulus oophorus on pre-treatment follicular ultrasound did not significantly affect cumulative pregnancy rate when ovulation-inducing medications were used.

source.title: Chapter 51: Effectiveness of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The pregnancy rates for these two groups of patients were basically the same" Section: Results of NaProTECHNOLOGY (cumulus oophorus comparison).

facts.claim: The NaProTECHNOLOGY fecundability rate in endometriosis was 3.11 versus 1.82 in the original Rock et al. 1981 conservative surgery analysis.

source.title: Chapter 51: Effectiveness of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the fecundability rate in endometriosis using NaProTECHNOLOGY approaches, the rate is 3.11. This compares to 1.82 in the original Rock analysis." Section: Results.

facts.claim: The NaProTECHNOLOGY fecundability rate in polycystic ovarian disease was 4.38 versus 1.34 in the Adashi/Rock ovarian wedge resection analysis.

source.title: Chapter 51: Effectiveness of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The fecundability rate with NaProTECHNOLOGY was 4.38 vs. 1.34 and, again, this is significantly different." Section: Results.

facts.claim: The NaProTECHNOLOGY infertility study population had an average age of 30.4 years and an average length of time trying to achieve pregnancy of 3.42 years.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A uterine manipulator should always be used when conducting a diagnostic laparoscopy." Section: Technical Considerations - Uterine Manipulator.

facts.claim: A 5-mm suprapubic probe should be used in all cases of diagnostic laparoscopy, along with irrigation and suction capability.

source.title: Chapter 63: Diagnostic Laparoscopy: "Near Contact" Approach
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A probe must be used with all diagnostic laparoscopies through the 5-mm suprapubic trocar. The ability to irrigate and suction must also be available." Section: Probe and Irrigation/Suction Capability.

facts.claim: Hilgers recommends that the surgeon develop a routine systematic sequence so that every diagnostic laparoscopy is performed in the same order, to avoid missing pelvic findings.

source.title: Chapter 63: Diagnostic Laparoscopy: "Near Contact" Approach
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "It is best for the surgeon to develop a technique which is routine so that each laparoscopy is performed in the same sequence." Section: NCL Technique.

facts.claim: In a 19-year-old case, the largest endometriosis lesion measured 400 microns in diameter, and 200 micron polypoid lesions were also documented.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In this 19-year-old, the largest lesion was 400 microns in diameter... The white light reflections on the top of the slide are 200μ polypoid lesions." Section: Figure 64-9.

facts.claim: Small visible lesions on the sigmoid colon often represent the 'tip of the iceberg' — easy to palpate but hard to see on inspection alone.

source.title: Chapter 64: Visual Appearance of Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW, Martin D, Redwine D, Reich H, Kresch A

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "What appears to be a small lesion on the sigmoid colon often represents the 'tip of the iceberg.' These can be easy to palpate but hard to see." Section: Figure 64-10.

facts.claim: Low power density CO₂ laser vaporization can leave carbon on top of residual endometriosis, particularly in areas like the broad ligament underlying the ovary, masking incomplete treatment.

source.title: Chapter 64: Visual Appearance of Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Scarred black areas in the pelvis are not always endometriosis — differential diagnosis is required rather than assuming pigmented lesions are disease.

source.title: Chapter 64: Visual Appearance of Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Scarred black areas are not always endometriosis." Section: Figure 64-13.

facts.claim: Clear and white vesicles on the fallopian tube are rarely endometriosis, but when such lesions are lateral to the tube, endometriosis becomes a plausible diagnosis.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Clear and white vesicles on the tube are rarely endometriosis. However, when the lesions are lateral to the tube, the diagnosis may be endometriosis." Section: Figure 64-14.

facts.claim: The visual-histologic correlation atlas of endometriosis was compiled by Dan Martin (Univ. Tennessee), David Redwine (St. Charles Medical Center, Bend OR), Harry Reich (Nesbit Memorial, Kingston PA), and Arnold Kresch (Stanford).

source.title: Chapter 64: Visual Appearance of Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Over the last 20 years, the various visual appearances of endometriosis have been confirmed with histologic confirmation through work by reproductive surgeons including Martin, Redwine, Reich, and Kresch.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: In white scarred endometriosis lesions, intravascular gland areas contain debris from bleeding giving brown/black coloration, but glands sit deep in fibrotic scar so hemosiderin may not be visible externally.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Black particles on the surface of white uterosacral ligament lesions can represent carbon residue from previous CO₂ laser vaporization rather than pigmented endometriosis itself.

source.title: Chapter 64: Visual Appearance of Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW, Martin D, Redwine D, Reich H, Kresch A

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This white lesion involves the uterosacral ligaments. The black particles on the surface are carbon from previous CO₂ laser vaporization." Section: Figure 64-3.

facts.claim: When white scarred areas are associated with red polyps, the red polyps are most commonly endometriosis, often with deeper glands and stroma in the underlying scar.

source.title: Chapter 64: Visual Appearance of Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW, Martin D, Redwine D, Reich H, Kresch A

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When white scarred areas are associated with red polyps, the red polyps are most commonly endometriosis." Section: Figure 64-4.

facts.claim: Clear polyps and vesicles may represent endometriosis, sometimes appearing as subtle vesicles with edema and little vascularization.

source.title: Chapter 64: Visual Appearance of Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW, Martin D, Redwine D, Reich H, Kresch A

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Clear polyps and vesicles may be endometriosis... Endometriosis can be seen as a subtle vesicle with some edema and little vascularization." Section: Figures 64-5/64-6.

facts.claim: Red polypoid endometriosis lesions range in size from 100 microns to 2 mm and can contain glands and stroma with variable vascularity and hemorrhage.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Red polypoid areas have been seen as small as 100 microns or as large as 2 mm." Section: Figures 64-7/64-8.

facts.claim: Teenagers commonly present with smaller clear polyps and small blebs as isolated findings of endometriosis.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: In patients with classic laparoscopic findings of salpingitis isthmica nodosa (SIN), indigo carmine dye can still transit through tubes that appear completely normal at laparoscopy, limiting laparoscopy's sensitivity for SIN diagnosis.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In spite of these classic findings of SIN, this dye will transit to tubes that laparoscopically appear to be perfectly normal." Section: Salpingitis Isthmica Nodosa.

facts.claim: SIN is identified laparoscopically by nodularity in the proximal tube, sometimes with an overlying powder burn appearance, and can be confirmed by indigo carmine extravasation into the nodules.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "indigo carmine dye is seen to extravasate into the large bilateral nodules of this patient with SIN" Section: Salpingitis Isthmica Nodosa.

facts.claim: Anterior rectum endometriosis is diagnosed by palpation with the suction probe to identify a nodule and by upward lifting of the uterine fundus by the surgical assistant to expose the cul-de-sac floor.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "with an upward lifting motion of the uterine fundus by the surgical assistant, the nodule of endometriosis on the anterior rectum is identified" Section: Endometriosis: Anterior Rectum.

facts.claim: Endometriosis on the terminal ileum can produce luminal narrowing secondary to serosal and mesenteric scarring, a small-bowel complication visible at laparoscopy.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Endometriosis on the terminal ileum (65-99) and its mesentery... some narrowing of the lumen is present secondary to the scarring that is present." Section: Endometriosis: Terminal Ileum.

facts.claim: Filmy adhesions covering the ovary are termed 'ovarian investment adhesions' and can involve omentum, tube, and ovary in complex masses.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When this type of adhesion covers the ovary, it is called an ovarian investment adhesion." Section: Pelvic Adhesions.

facts.claim: Even with extensive periovarian and peritubal adhesions obliterating the cul-de-sac, if the distal tube and fimbria appear normal after adhesiolysis, the prognosis for pregnancy after surgical repair remains favorable.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "distal tube and fimbria appear normal, suggesting repair of this condition would hold definite hope for pregnancy." Section: Pelvic Adhesions.

facts.claim: Some but not all PCO patients exhibit a dilated surface blood vessel pattern on the ovary visible at laparoscopy, making this a supportive but non-universal sign.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The dilated, surface blood vessel pattern observed on some, but not all, patients with PCO." Section: Polycystic Ovaries.

facts.claim: Walthard rests are classic fallopian tube anomalies appearing as multiple small blister-like vesicles on the medial surface of the tube.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Classic Walthard rests (multiple small blister-like vesicles) on the medial surface of the fallopian tube." Section: Fallopian Tube Anomalies.

facts.claim: At the heart of Surgical NaProTECHNOLOGY is the ability to make a correct diagnosis, which is the stated purpose of developing diagnostic laparoscopic skill.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "At the heart of Surgical NaProTECHNOLOGY is the ability to make a correct diagnosis." Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Elongated fimbria ovarica are defined as fimbrial structures measuring greater than 5 cm, and may be present bilaterally in the same patient.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Elongated fimbria ovarica of the right (65-143) and left (65-144) tube in the same patient. Each measures > 5 cm." Section: Fallopian Tube Anomalies.

facts.claim: Peritubal cysts can reach 5 cm in size and may present as a cystic adnexal mass on initial evaluation before being identified as of tubal rather than ovarian origin.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A cystic mass in the left adnexa (65-148) is a large 5 cm peritubal cyst" Section: Fallopian Tube Anomalies.

facts.claim: Accessory fallopian tubes are a tubal anomaly in which additional tube structures arise from the lateral aspect of the tubal ampulla.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Two accessory fallopian tubes coming off the lateral aspect of the right tubal ampulla." Section: Fallopian Tube Anomalies.

facts.claim: Fimbrial assessment is performed by immersing the fimbria under Ringer's lactate solution at laparoscopy, a technique that allows detailed study of fimbrial architecture.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "By immersing the fimbria under water (Ringer's lactate) at the time of laparoscopy, one can study them" Section: Fimbria Under Water.

facts.claim: Black pepper spots on the posterior bladder are consistent with carbonization secondary to previous laser vaporization treatment, distinguishing prior treatment artifact from active endometriosis.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "black pepper spots are seen on the right and are consistent with carbonization secondary to previous laser vaporization." Section: Endometriosis: Posterior Bladder.

facts.claim: Hemosiderin deposits appearing as golden brown tissue on the posterior bladder are a result of endometriosis and serve as a diagnostic visual marker.

source.title: Chapter 65: Atlas of Findings in Diagnostic Laparoscopy
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "there is also a larger hemosiderin deposit (the golden brown tissue) which is the result of endometriosis." Section: Endometriosis: Posterior Bladder.

facts.claim: Endometriosis of the rectosigmoid colon can appear as small dark lesions with white halo, white scarified lesions, red blister lesions, red lesions on epiploic fat pads, and puckered lesions on fat tags.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Dense bilateral ovarian endometriomas can become adherent to each other and to the lower posterior uterus, forming a combined adnexal-uterine mass visible at laparoscopy.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The findings of endometriosis in peritoneal pockets of this kind is so common that it is to be expected." Section: Endometriosis: Peritoneal Pockets.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When the numbers 6, 8, and 10 are used, a letter from the right hand column must always be used." Section: VDRS.

facts.claim: Special Peak-type mucus categories are recorded as 10DL (damp with lubrication), 10SL (shiny with lubrication), and 10WL (wet with lubrication); lubrication denotes Peak-type regardless of stretch.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "10DL (damp with lubrication), 10SL (shiny with lubrication), and 10WL (wet with lubrication). Since lubrication is present" Section: VDRS.

facts.claim: Frequency of the most fertile sign is recorded as X1 (once), X2 (twice), X3 (three times), or AD (all day, meaning four or more observations).

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "X1 = seen only once that day, X2 = seen twice that day, X3 = seen three times that day, and AD = seen all day." Section: VDRS.

facts.claim: Once mucus has been observed four times in a day, no further count is kept; it automatically becomes an all-day (AD) observation in the VDRS.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Once it has been observed four times, the woman no longer needs to keep a count... It automatically becomes an all-day (AD) observation." Section: VDRS.

facts.claim: The CrMS client should chart the most fertile sign of the day at the end of each day, including days of menstruation.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The client should chart the proper stamp and description at the end of each day during the cycle including the days of menstruation." Section: Basic Principles of Good Charting.

facts.claim: CrMS teachers read charts day-by-day reading each description and verifying stamp placement; medical consultants instead read charts globally, looking for patterns meeting specific criteria.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Read the chart 'globally,' not 'day by day' although the message of the chart is still in the daily descriptions." Section: Principles of Chart Reading for the Medical Consultant.

facts.claim: The CrMS medical consultant globally assesses cycle length/regularity, pre- and post-Peak phase length, Peak Day occurrence, mucus intensity/length, continuous discharge, and abnormal bleeding.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The cycle length and regularity. The length of the pre- and post-Peak phase. The occurrence of the Peak Day" Section: Principles of Chart Reading for the Medical Consultant.

facts.claim: In the CrMS example 28-day cycle, menstrual flow was 5 days, dry days totaled 19, and both pre-Peak and post-Peak phases were 14 days each.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the length of the cycle is 28 days in duration. The length of the menstrual flow is five days and the dry days are 19 days long" Section: Table 7-10.

facts.claim: Red pencil is used for all CrMS chart corrections at follow-up, and corrected stamps are placed at an angle to identify them as follow-up corrections.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A red pencil is used during chart correcting... When stamps appear on a chart at an angle, it means that those were corrections" Section: Basic Chart Correcting.

facts.claim: In the CrMS, pasty-cloudy (PC) days during the post-Peak phase should be charted with white baby stamps in the early learning process, not green stamps.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "pasty, cloudy days during the post-Peak phase of the cycle should be charted with white, baby stamps." Section: Errors Found and Corrected.

facts.claim: During the first month of charting or during the first complete cycle (whichever is shorter), the CrMS client should avoid genital contact so mucus is observed in its natural state without seminal fluid influence.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "During the first month of charting or during the first complete cycle (whichever is shorter), the client should avoid genital contact" Section: Basic Principles of Good Charting.

facts.claim: Whenever mucus can be stretched in the CrMS, a color must be observed and recorded; a stretch observation without color is considered incomplete.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Whenever the mucus can be stretched, a color should be observed and recorded." Section: Errors Found and Corrected.

facts.claim: If lubrication is still present the day after an initially marked Peak, the Peak Day was misidentified and must be relocated to the last lubricative day.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "with lubrication still present on day 15, the client has inaccurately identified the Peak Day." Section: Errors Found and Corrected.

facts.claim: The CrMS normal 29-day working example chart has 6 days of menstrual flow, a 5-day mucus cycle, four pre-Peak dry days, and a 13-day post-Peak phase.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "29-day cycle in which the length of the menstrual flow is six days and the length of the mucus cycle is five days... post-Peak phase is 13 days." Section: Table 7-13.

facts.claim: On light and very light days of menstrual flow in the CrMS, an observation of mucus (dry or mucus status) should always be recorded in addition to the flow symbol.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "During the light and very light days of the menstrual flow, an observation of the mucus should always be recorded." Section: VDRS.

facts.claim: When a CrMS client observes multiple discharges of differing fertility, only the most fertile sign of the day is charted; less-fertile observations are struck through with a red line during correction.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the user has not recorded 'the most fertile sign.' Since 10CL... would be considered more fertile than 8C/K... a red line is drawn through" Section: Errors Found and Corrected.

facts.claim: The CrMS chart provides space for six months of charting, with cycle day numbers 1 through 35 running across the top of the inside of the chart.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "there are numbers across the top from 1 through 35... There is room for six months of charting." Section: Inside of the Chart.

facts.claim: CrMS uses four basic stamp types: plain red for bleeding days, plain green for infertile dry days, white baby stamps for mucus days, and green baby stamps for dry days within the count of 3.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Plain Red Stamps = For days of bleeding... Green, Baby Stamps = For dry days that are fertile (within the count of 3)" Section: Stamps.

facts.claim: Yellow stamps in the CrMS are only used upon specific indication and with teacher advice; they are not given to new clients at the introductory session.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "yellow stamps are only used upon specific indication and then only with the advice of the teacher" Section: Stamps.

facts.claim: On the CrMS chart, 'P' marks the Peak Day on a white baby stamp, numbers '1, 2, 3' mark the three stamps following Peak Day, and 'I' marks an act of intercourse.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "P = Placed on a white, baby stamp on the Peak Day... 1, 2, 3 = Placed on the three stamps following the Peak Day... I = An act of intercourse" Section: Descriptions.

facts.claim: In CrMS menstrual flow notation: H = heavy, M = moderate, L = light, VL = very light (or spotting), B = brown or black bleeding.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "H = heavy, M = moderate, L = light, VL = very light (or spotting), and B = brown (or black) are used." Section: VDRS.

facts.claim: The Peak Day is the last day of mucus discharge that is clear, stretchy, or lubricative and can only be marked on the day after the Peak, once a change in mucus has occurred.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "That last day of the mucus discharge that is clear, stretchy, or lubricative is called the Peak Day" Section: A Charting Example.

facts.claim: In the VDRS, the numbers 0, 2, 2W, and 4 all represent observations with the same significance as dry observations for stamp placement purposes.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The numbers 0, 2, 2W, and 4 all relate to observations that have the same significance as dry observations." Section: VDRS.

facts.claim: PEARS (Pelvic Excision and Repair Surgery) is defined as plastic reconstructive pelvic surgery whose primary intent is to remove disease from pelvic organs and repair them so as not to form pelvic adhesions.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "PEARS (Pelvic Excision and Repair Surgery) is a form of plastic reconstructive surgery of the pelvis whose primary intent is to remove disease" Section: Overview.

facts.claim: For very large retroperitoneal space defects in the posterior cul-de-sac, concentric purse-string suture placements of 2-0 Chromic are used to close the dead space.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "use concentric, purse string suture placements of 2-0 Chromic to close the dead space" Section: Posterior Cul-de-sac.

facts.claim: For small endometriomas, the preferred cutting instrument is CO2 laser at 20 watts of continuous energy power because it causes slightly less peripheral damage than micro-monopolar cautery.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "CO2 laser at 20 watts of continuous energy power for the circumferential incision. The CO2 laser causes slightly less peripheral damage" Section: Small Endometrioma.

facts.claim: Ovarian cortex after small endometrioma excision is closed with 5-0 Prolene in a running imbricating-type suture, leaving smooth, glistening surfaces with no abraded surfaces.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Close with 5-0 Prolene in a running imbricating-type suture, leaving smooth, glistening surfaces with no abraded surfaces" Section: Small Endometrioma.

facts.claim: After PEARS, the pelvis is irrigated with Ringer's lactate containing 5000 units of heparin, with the goal of obtaining a completely clear field to confirm hemostasis.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the pelvis is irrigated with Ringer's lactate containing 5000 units of heparin. The goal is to obtain a completely clear field" Section: Irrigation and Hemostasis Verification.

facts.claim: If the irrigation fluid does not completely clear, a small bleeding area is almost universally present, requiring a figure-of-eight suture of 4-0 Prolene for control.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If the fluid will not completely clear, there is almost universally a small bleeding area somewhere within the surgical repair field." Section: Irrigation and Hemostasis Verification.

facts.claim: When the ovary is adhered to the lateral pelvic wall, the best dissecting tool is the operating surgeon's fingers using oscillating finger dissection, as blunt dissection is least likely to cause major trauma.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The best dissecting tool for removing the ovary off the lateral pelvic wall is the operating surgeon's fingers." Section: Severe Endometriosis with Pelvic Wall Adhesions.

facts.claim: For large endometriomas, the circumferential incision uses CO2 laser at 20 watts continuous, and the complete endometrioma including its capsule must be removed to prevent recurrence in that ovary.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The complete endometrioma needs to be removed including its capsule. This is extremely important in preventing the recurrence" Section: Large Endometrioma.

facts.claim: The ovarian medulla after large endometrioma excision is reapproximated using figure-of-eight 2-0 Chromic sutures, followed by cortex closure with 5-0 Prolene running suture inverting the rough edges.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Reapproximate the ovarian medulla using figure-of-eight 2-0 Chromic sutures" Section: Large Endometrioma.

facts.claim: Large peritoneal defects left after endometrioma dissection from the lateral pelvic wall must be repaired by reapproximating with 4-0 Prolene running imbricating suture, inverting all cut edges.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "it leaves a large gap in the peritoneum. In all cases, this peritoneum must be repaired." Section: Peritoneal Defect Repair.

facts.claim: The PEARS closure technique can be applied during pregnancy; a large left-ovary endometrioma was removed at approximately 16 weeks gestation using identical closure technique.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A large endometrioma in the left ovary of a woman about 16 weeks gestation was removed." Section: Endometrioma During Pregnancy.

facts.claim: PEARS is applied to endometriosis of the ovary, peritoneum, bladder, large and small intestine, polycystic ovarian disease, extensive pelvic adhesive disease, and tubal surgery.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Endometriosis of the ovary, peritoneum, bladder, large and small intestine" Section: Applications of PEARS.

facts.claim: Dermoid cyst ovarian repair uses 2-0 Chromic figure-of-eight sutures on the undersurface, then PEARS cortex closure with 5-0 Prolene running imbricating suture inverting edges to leave no rough surface.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Repair the undersurface of the ovary with 2-0 Chromic figure-of-eight sutures" Section: Dermoid Cyst.

facts.claim: PEARS combined with good hemostasis and anti-adhesion barriers such as Interceed and Hyskon provides excellent results in adhesion reduction and restoration of functional tissue integrity.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the use of anti-adhesion barriers such as Interceed and Hyskon will provide excellent results in the reduction of adhesions" Section: Final Comment.

facts.claim: Imbricating suture technique inserts the needle into the peritoneum to exit prior to the edge on one side, and external to the edge on the other side, causing the peritoneum to invert naturally.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Insert the suture into the peritoneum on one side with the needle coming out prior to reaching the edge." Section: Imbricating Suture Technique.

facts.claim: PEARS operating time ranges from 2 to 6 hours depending on the nature of the conditions being treated.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The range of operating time can be anywhere from 2 to 6 hours, depending upon the nature of the conditions being treated." Section: Surgical Principles.

facts.claim: The core PEARS principle uses either CO2 laser or micro-monopolar cautery as a cutting instrument with removal of the diseased peritoneum or ovary, inert suture such as Prolene, and careful inversion of tissue edges.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The use of either CO2 laser or micro-monopolar cautery as a cutting instrument and removal of the diseased portion of the peritoneum or ovary." Section: General Operating Principles.

facts.claim: For peritoneal endometriosis excision, a non-traumatic incision is made at least a few millimeters surrounding the area to remove all disease.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a non-traumatic incision is made in the peritoneum surrounding the area of endometriosis giving at least a few millimeters surrounding the area" Section: Anterior Uterus and Posterior Bladder.

facts.claim: Hemostasis after peritoneal endometriosis removal uses 2-0 Chromic on an HL-6 needle, either as figure-of-eight sutures or to ligate specific blood vessels.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Using 2-0 Chromic on an HL-6 needle is very helpful. Either figure-of-eight 2-0 Chromic sutures can be used" Section: Anterior Uterus and Posterior Bladder.

facts.claim: The peritoneum is repaired using 4-0 Prolene, inverting the edges internally so that only smooth, glistening peritoneum is apparent on the surface.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The peritoneum is repaired using 4-0 Prolene, inverting the edges internally so that only smooth, glistening peritoneum is apparent" Section: Anterior Uterus and Posterior Bladder.

facts.claim: When excising endometriosis from the broad ligament or lateral pelvic wall, the peritoneum is grasped with a Stille line toothed hemoscope and pulled away from vital structures (periuterine veins, uterine artery, ureter).

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Pull the peritoneum away from the vital structures underneath (periuterine veins, uterine artery, ureter)" Section: Broad Ligament and Lateral Pelvic Wall.

facts.claim: Brisk bleeding in the posterior broad ligament is controlled with a Stille clamp and a stick tie of 2-0 Chromic.

source.title: Chapter 70: PEARS: Peritoneal and Ovarian Endometriosis
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "place a Stille clamp around it (bleeding is more brisk) and use a stick tie of 2-0 Chromic" Section: Broad Ligament and Lateral Pelvic Wall.

facts.claim: The rationale for the two-application laparoscopic appendectomy technique is that the appendix is considerably larger than the mesoappendix, and the Endostapler places two triple-staggered lines of staples, anticipating less bleeding.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The Endostapler places two triple-staggered lines of staples." Section: Rationale.

facts.claim: The most common location for small bowel endometriosis is the terminal ileum.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The most common location for small bowel endometriosis is the terminal ileum." Section: Small Bowel (Terminal Ileum).

facts.claim: Superficial terminal ileum endometriosis lesions are excised using microcautery at 10 watts superficially through the serosa, lifted with Cushing pickups, and closed with interrupted 5-0 Prolene in a transverse fashion.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Incision: Microcautery at 10 watts, very superficial through serosa" Section: Technique for Superficial Lesions.

facts.claim: If bowel lumen is entered during excision of terminal ileum endometriosis, superficial serosal closure is inadequate and a general surgeon must be consulted.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If excision does not enter the bowel lumen, this closure is adequate. If lumen is entered, consult a general surgeon" Section: Technique for Superficial Lesions.

facts.claim: For superficial mesenteric endometriosis lesions near the terminal ileum, closure is done with a 5-0 Prolene purse-string suture because the mesenteric serosa has more mobility than bowel serosa.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Mesenteric serosa has more mobility → use purse-string suture of 5-0 Prolene" Section: Mesentery and Cecum Lesions.

facts.claim: Rectosigmoid colon endometriosis lesions are excised by making a circumferential, superficial incision through the serosa with microcautery, grasping and tenting the lesion with Cushing forceps, then excising completely with microcautery.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Make circumferential, superficial incision through serosa using microcautery" Section: Rectosigmoid Colon, Technique.

facts.claim: When removing and imbricating epiploic fat tags containing endometriosis on the rectosigmoid colon, the arteriole feeding the fat tag must be securely ligated.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Remember there is an arteriole feeding the fat tag. When removing and imbricating, the vessel must be securely ligated." Section: Closure.

facts.claim: Counter-traction is considered extremely important for excising anterior rectal endometriosis nodules without entering the lumen, achieved with either a long Allis clamp or a 2-0 Chromic suture placed through the nodule using a UR-6 slimly angulated needle.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Counter-traction is extremely important for excising nodules without entering the lumen." Section: Approach 1, Counter-traction.

facts.claim: During anterior rectal nodule excision, the dissection will often extend into the muscular portion of the rectum but the lesion can be enucleated using counter-traction, and residual fibrotic scarring is palpated and removed with a long Allis or Stille clamp plus microcautery.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Excision will often go into muscular portion but can be enucleated using counter-traction" Section: Approach 1, Excision.

facts.claim: Anterior rectal endometriosis excision without entering the lumen is closed in a two-layer fashion, distinct from the single superficial serosal closure used for terminal ileum or rectosigmoid superficial lesions.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Step 4 - Two-Layer Closure:" Section: Approach 1.

facts.claim: Approximately 20 percent of appendiceal endometriosis lesions are detected only by palpation and may be missed by visual inspection alone, which is the rationale some cite for incidental appendectomy in patients with severe endometriosis.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "approximately 20 percent of appendiceal lesions are detected only by palpation and may be missed by visual inspection alone." Section: Appendectomy.

facts.claim: Bowel endometriosis lesions covered in PEARS techniques are generally considered mild, but may cause considerable pain and may contribute to infertility or miscarriages.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Each lesion presented would generally be considered mild, but may cause considerable pain and may contribute to infertility or miscarriages." Section: Procedures Covered.

facts.claim: Hilgers' recommendation is to remove the appendix only if there is obvious or potential endometriosis present, not as a routine incidental procedure.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Author's Recommendation: Remove the appendix only if there is obvious endometriosis or potential endometriosis present" Section: Appendectomy.

facts.claim: Appendiceal blood supply comes from terminal branches of the ileocolic artery or branches from the anastomotic arcades, and there may be multiple appendiceal arteries within the mesoappendix that must be secured during appendectomy.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Comes from terminal branches of the ileocolic artery or branches from the anastomotic arcades" Section: Appendiceal Blood Supply.

facts.claim: In the PEARS laparotomy appendectomy technique, the mesoappendix is divided with hemostats and Metzenbaum scissors, and each clamped portion is tied securely with 2-0 Chromic or 2-0 Vicryl free tie.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Tie each clamped portion securely with 2-0 Chromic or 2-0 Vicryl free tie" Section: Laparotomy Technique, Step 1.

facts.claim: In PEARS appendectomy, after removing the appendix the stump is cleansed with Betadine, then closed with a 2-0 Vicryl free tie under the first clamp followed by a second 2-0 Vicryl tie placed distally through the subserosal surface.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Place second 2-0 Vicryl tie distal to first, securing through subserosal surface" Section: Stump Closure.

facts.claim: A distinguishing feature of PEARS laparotomy appendectomy is retroperitonealization of the appendiceal stump, unlike most surgeons who do not bury the stump.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "PEARS Principle: Unlike most surgeons who do not bury the stump, in PEARS it is best to:" Section: PEARS Retroperitonealization.

facts.claim: The PEARS laparoscopic appendectomy uses the Endopath ELC35 stapling device through a 12 mm trocar in the left lower quadrant, applied in two stages: across the mesoappendix and across the base of the appendix.

source.title: Chapter 71: PEARS for Bowel Endometriosis: Surgical Techniques for the Gynecologist
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Endopath® ELC35 stapling device through 12 mm trocar in left lower quadrant" Section: Laparoscopic Technique.

facts.claim: In NaProTECHNOLOGY pregnancy confirmation, a quantitative B-HCG result over 50 confirms pregnancy; if positive but less than 50, it is repeated in 48 hours to assess whether it is increasing or decreasing.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If the result is over 50, the pregnancy is confirmed. If the result is positive, but less than 50, it should be repeated in 48 hours" Section: For gynecological patients.

facts.claim: In NaProTECHNOLOGY infertility care, patients with a history of infertility or miscarriage who confirm pregnancy are started on progesterone supplementation; those with three or more miscarriages are also started on HCG supplementation.

source.title: Chapter 85: NaProTECHNOLOGY Nursing
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If the patient has had three or more miscarriages, she is also started on HCG supplementation." Section: For gynecological patients.

facts.claim: For HCG dilution at the Pope Paul VI Institute, a 10,000-unit vial diluted with 10 cc of diluent yields a strength of 100 units per 0.1 cc (used for 200 units per 0.2 cc dosing).

facts.claim: For HCG IM injections, the dorsogluteal site is used (upper outer quadrant of the buttock, 2-3 inches below the crest of the ileum), with the needle inserted at a 90-degree angle.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When used correctly, the CrMS is over 99 percent effective as a system of avoiding pregnancy." Section: 14 Fundamental Principles.

facts.claim: Unusual bleeding is treated as Peak fertility plus three days after the last day of unusual bleeding.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Bleeding other than normal menstruation is considered Peak fertility plus three days after the last day of unusual bleeding." Section: D-6.

facts.claim: Post-Peak infertility begins on the fourth day post-Peak, always at the end of the day, allowing ovum disintegration and detection of any resumed Peak-type mucus.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Infertility begins on the fourth day post-Peak, always end of the day." Section: E-3.

facts.claim: Seminal fluid can discharge for up to 72 hours after intercourse and may resemble Peak-type mucus.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Seminal fluid can discharge for up to 72 hours after intercourse and may resemble Peak-type mucus, causing confusion." Section: F. Seminal Fluid Instruction.

facts.claim: The Seminal Fluid Instruction directs the woman to urinate within one hour of intercourse, bear down with alternating Kegel exercises, and wipe until seminal fluid is gone.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Urinate after intercourse (within one hour)" and "Bear down and do several Kegel exercises in alternating fashion" Section: The Seminal Fluid Instruction.

facts.claim: During fertile time, couples should wait 30 minutes before using the Seminal Fluid Instruction to allow sperm migration to the fallopian tubes; SFI is not contraceptive.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "During fertile time: Wait 30 minutes before using SFI. Allows sperm time to migrate to fallopian tubes. SFI is not contraceptive." Section: Timing Details.

facts.claim: Any observation after the Seminal Fluid Instruction is never assumed to be seminal fluid but always assumed to be cervical mucus.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Whatever is observed is never assumed to be seminal fluid—it is always assumed to be cervical mucus and instructions are followed accordingly." Section: Key Principle.

facts.claim: A preliminary survey of 54 pregnancies using Peak fertility days showed over 75% achieved pregnancy in the very first cycle.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A preliminary survey of 54 pregnancies showed over 75% became pregnant in the very first cycle of using fertile days" Section: Achieving Pregnancy.

facts.claim: Ovulation occurs in 95% of cycles from P-2 to P+2 (two days before to two days after the Peak Day).

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Ovulation occurs in 95% of cycles from P-2 to P+2." Section: Special Fertility Instruction.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Ovulation, if it occurs, is associated with the last Peak Day" Section: The Peak Day vs. a Peak day.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Pre-Peak dry days are infertile, always end of the day only, alternate days initially (every other day)." Section: E-1.

facts.claim: The Pre-Peak Phase is measured from the first day of menstruation through and including the Peak Day.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Pre-Peak Phase: From the first day of menstruation up to and including the Peak Day." Section: Cycle Phase Definitions.

facts.claim: The Post-Peak Phase runs from the day after the Peak Day through the last day before the next menstruation.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Post-Peak Phase: From the day after the Peak Day through the last day prior to the beginning of the next menstruation." Section: Cycle Phase Definitions.

facts.claim: A mucus patch greater than seven days of non-Peak-type mucus is referred to as a continuous discharge of non-Peak-type mucus.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "If greater than seven days, referred to as a continuous discharge of non-Peak-type mucus." Section: Core Mucus Definitions.

facts.claim: Peak Day identification is based on mucus character (clear, stretchy, or lubricative), not on mucus amount; any one of the three signs alone qualifies.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY

facts.claim: Independent predictors of a successful THB included: maternal age between 18 and 30 years (OR: 12.4, 95% CI: 6.6-23.2, p < 0.001); consultation for recurrent pregnancy loss (OR: 2.7, 95% CI: 1.7-4.3, p < 0.001); a previous infertility duration of 7-12 months (OR: 2.7, 95% CI: 1.9-3.8, p < 0.001).
facts.body: Independent predictors of THB in multivariate analysis (N=1,310): maternal age 18-30 years (OR: 12.4, 95% CI: 6.6-23.2, p<0.001), recurrent pregnancy loss as infertility type (OR: 2.7, 95% CI: 1.7-4.3, p<0.001), prior infertility duration 7-12 months (OR: 2.7, 95% CI: 1.9-3.8, p<0.001), endometriosis diagnosis (OR: 1.6), functional disorders (OR: 1.5), male factor (OR: 1.5), endometrial disorders (OR: 1.4). Younger age is by far the strongest single predictor.

facts.claim: A sensitivity analysis modeled three scenarios: (A) 50% lower pregnancy probability among dropouts, (B) equal probability, and (C) 50% higher. Resulting adjusted cumulative pregnancy rates were 47.7%, 62.1%, and 76.5%, respectively.
facts.body: The headline 62.1% adjusted cumulative THB rate depends on the standard Kaplan-Meier non-informative censoring assumption. Sensitivity analysis modeled three scenarios: (A) dropouts have 50% lower pregnancy probability: 47.7%; (B) equal probability (base case): 62.1%; (C) 50% higher probability: 76.5%. The pessimistic scenario (47.7%) still substantially exceeds the crude rate (35.3%). Use the 47.7%-62.1% range for honest reporting.

facts.claim: The cumulative withdrawal rate reached 41.5%, with 21.4% of couples discontinuing treatment during the first year and 37.6% by the end of the second year. The main reasons for withdrawal in the first year were discouragement (31.5%) and transition to ART (17.1%).
facts.body: Overall withdrawal rate: 41.5%. First-year withdrawal: 21.4%. Main first-year reasons: discouragement (31.5%) and transition to ART (17.1%). Second-year: treatment completion (41.4%) was the primary reason. The overall censored rate was 64.7%. Dropout rates (37-48% at 12 months) are consistent across all published NPT studies. Similar dropout rates (45-69%) have been reported for ART.

facts.body: When looking at CrMS, a major identifiable marker is the variation in the length of the post-Peak phase of the cycle. This variation in the length of the post-Peak phase (which is usually a sign of significantly suboptimal progesterone production) was statistically significant (p<.0001).

facts.claim: progesterone is given on days 12, 15, and 18 in the first cycle. In the second cycle, it is given on days 15, 18, and 21 and in the third cycle on days 18, 21, and 24.
facts.body: Generally speaking, the prescription of progesterone should begin prior to the onset of her next anticipated menstrual flow. Thus, in a situation such as Figure 32-20, the progesterone should begin on day 12 or 14 in the first cycle of administration. The progesterone is then given on days 12, 15, and 18 in the first cycle. In the second cycle, it is given on days 15, 18, and 21 and in the third cycle on days 18, 21, and 24. After three cycles of intramuscular progesterone used in this fashion, the progesterone feeds back to the hypothalamus and restructures the hypothalamic pituitary ovarian axis.

facts.body: If a menstrual period does not occur by day 35 and the patient is not pregnant, then 200 mg of progesterone is administered IM to initiate a period. Once the menstrual flow begins, the dosage is increased to 100 mg by mouth every day (QD) day 5 through 9 of that cycle. If a period does not occur by day 35 and pregnancy or ovulation has not occurred, then 200 mg of progesterone is once again given IM to initiate a menstrual flow. The dosage is then increased to 150 mg by mouth every day (PO QD) day 5 through 9 of the cycle.

The patients who had adherent placentas had elevated progesterone levels during the first trimester of pregnancy but they became significantly decreased in the second trimester. Those patients who had a low-lying placenta and a placenta previa (as identified by early ultrasound) and a midtrimester abruption or subchorionic hemorrhage (also identified by ultrasound) had significantly lower progesterone levels both in the second and the third trimester of pregnancy.

facts.body: The standard dosage used begins with 0.25 mg by mouth four times a day (PO QID) for 10 days and then increases to 0.5 mg QID x 10 days, then 1 mg QID x 10 days and then 2 mg QID x 10 days. For many years, the protocol did not go higher than 1 mg QID. However, recent data (see below) suggests that the dosage should go higher if a good effect is to be obtained. Thus, once 2 mg QID is reached, the dosage would be increased even further to 4 mg QID, then 6 mg QID, then 12 mg every day at bedtime (QD hs) and then 50 mg QD hs.

facts.body: It has been shown that a low-dose, preovulatory implementation of human menopausal gonadotropin therapy can be effective in the treatment of those patients with regular menstrual cycles and poor cervical mucus. This program utilized one ampule (75 IU) of HMG from days 5 through 11 of the cycle. If there was no response, the dosage was increased to two ampules. In this case, because these women were having regular cycles to begin with, the use of HCG was not necessary to insure ovulation. This HMG protocol was found to be more efficacious than IUI, IVF or GIFT.
